subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: An insurance policy pays for a random loss $X$ subject to a deductible of $C$, where $0 < C < 1$. The loss amount is modeled as a continuous random variable with density function $f(x) = \begin{cases} 2x & 0 < x < 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ Given a random loss $X$, the probability that the insurance payment is less than 0.5 is equal to 0.64. Calculate $C$.
